content.value: UW Diary Cards is a digital tool that helps students easily complete their weekly diary cards, a requirement in many UW EDUC courses. For their homework assignment, students fill out daily report cards, allowing them to easily reflect on how effective a skill was, reinforcing their learning, and actively changing the habits of students. They can also add custom notes for the teacher if they have additional questions on course content. This software effectively replaces the traditional paper diary cards, which are often forgotten, lost, or guiltily filled out inconsistently.

items.value: **Download as a Word document**, formatted with a maintainable template within the source code.
**One-click email sending** to either share directly with faculty, or send to themselves and download to submit onto Canvas.

content.value: The app streamlines the entire process—making diary card tracking faster, easier, more accessible, and convenient. The app is designed not to replace the paper method, but to output and follow the same format as traditional diary cards. It purely works as an alternative for students who prefer digital tools. The app is built using Flutter and Dart, ensuring cross-platform compatibility and a smooth user experience on both Android and iOS devices.

images.alt: Home screen with name and date at top, which below shows a list of diary cards representing each week
User interface of the skill screen where the user hits check boxes to indicate completion of skills

content.value: This personal website is a showcase of my accomplishments, to be viewed by recruiters and peers. It integrates recent graphic design knowledge and experiments with a unique layout using buttons instead of a classic app bar. It is built with HTML and styled using Tailwind CSS for a clean, responsive design. Each project includes short, clear descriptions, and all content is managed through a structured JSON file.

items.value: **Dynamic web interface** for different screen sizes and devices. A website that looks good on mobile, tablet, and desktop.
**Custom components**, originally designed in figma, it was then translated into code using Tailwind CSS for a unique and modern look.
**Easy to maintain**, it uses a modular structure in the form of a JSON format to easily create new projects and edit existing ones.

content.value: This website serves as a personal portfolio, highlighting my skills and projects in coding, design, and research. It is hosted on GitHub Pages for easy access and sharing.

I developed Collect Twirling Bits, a reusable TypeScript npm package that lets websites add interactive, collectible star bits to the page with minimal setup. The package exposes a simple createStarBits API that handles spawning, animation, collection, persistent scoring, and cleanup. I designed it so it could be installed into any frontend project as a lightweight visual interaction layer, while still allowing developers to customize spawn rate, maximum objects on screen, star size, animation speed, storage key, and whether star bits are allowed to appear over images.

items.value: **Data pipeline**: Pipelined data from multiple sources, including mRNA expression datasets and histopathology image repositories to get it in a readable format for a CNN.
**Model Development**: Built and trained a neural network using TensorFlow to map mRNA data to corresponding histopathology images.
**Evaluation**: Assessed model performance using metrics such as AUC and accuracy.

content.value: The project demonstrated the potential of machine learning in biomedical research, coming back with unexpectedly high positives on a limited (180 patient) dataset. ORA12, a gene which has an association according to the Human Protein Atlas, but is not fully understood in the context of lung cancer, had an AUC of 0.298 and an accuracy of 0.778. Much better results than anticipated, providing stronger evidence of ORA12's potential role as a biomarker. This suggests that my CNN model and methodology could aid basic research on cancer biomarkers.

images.alt: Training and validation loss graph over several epochs, some stopped early due to early stopping in place. Shows a decreasing trend in both training and validation loss.
AUC results of the model, showing the top 10 genes with the highest AUC scores, along with their accuracy.

content.value: This is a reusable TypeScript npm package that lets websites add interactive, collectible star bits to the page with minimal setup.

items.value: **Reusable npm package**: Published as collect-twirling-bits, users can install and run on any frontend project.
**Configurable API**: Designed options for spawn rate, maximum bits on screen, star size, spin speed, storage key, and image-overlap behavior.
**Interactive DOM behavior**: Built collectible star bits that spawn on the page, animate continuously, update a persistent counter, and clean up safely.
**Collision-aware placement**: Added logic to avoid spawning over text, images, existing star bits, and screen edges.
**Publishing workflow**: Configured build output, package exports, type declarations, local tarball testing, and npm publishing setup.

content.value: This project demonstrates my ability to create packages and design modular projects that can fit together into larger applications. It also shows my understanding of DOM manipulation, animation, and user interaction in web development.
